metadata.notes: Voice rewrite — conversational walkthrough style matching Wingspan gold standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, RulesPal, Dized rules, BGG forums.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
- 1 double-sided game board — use the 1–3 player side or the 4–5 player side depending on your count
- 5 asymmetric hive mats, each with hexagonal tile slots and resource storage spaces
- 5 docking mats with a Landing Area and an Active Pool section

#### Tiles (129 total, all unique)
- **Farm tiles** (hexagonal) — give you resource storage and income every time you retrieve workers
- **Recruit tiles** (hexagonal) — grant ongoing abilities that fire when you take specific actions
- **Development tiles** (hexagonal) — one-time immediate benefits when built
- **Carving tiles** — major end-game scoring based on conditions; limited supply, never replenished once taken
- **Planet tiles** — shuffled face-down, revealed when you explore
- **Dance tiles** — placed on Convert action spaces at setup
- **20 faction tiles** (double-sided: basic and upgraded) — each gives your hive a unique asymmetric power
- **Frame tiles** — 4-hex extensions you can bolt onto your hive mat for extra building space

#### Cards
- 45 seed cards — play them for one-time benefits, discard them for 1 basic resource, or plant them for end-game scoring
- 14 Automa cards (solo mode)

#### Tokens & Workers
- Resources come in 5 types: Pollen, Fiber, Water (basic); Wax, Honey (advanced — you can only get these through conversion or special effects)
- 35 hibernation tokens (7 per player)
- Worker bee cubes whose strength ranges from 1 to 4 — you track this by rotating the cube to show the current face
- 1 QueenShip miniature (shared by all players, moves on the exploration map)
- Explore tokens, dance tokens, and player tracking tokens

#### Other
- Rulebook, Automa rulebook, Appendix (tile reference), Teaching guide, 5 player aids

**Checkpoint:** Make sure each player has a hive mat, a docking mat, their faction tile (basic side up), starting workers in the Active Pool, starting resources placed on hive storage spots, 7 hibernation tokens on their docking mat, and 1 seed card in hand. The shared board should show the correct side for your player count, with farm/recruit/development tiles in rows, carving tiles on Carve spaces, dance tiles on Convert spaces, and the QueenShip on its starting location.

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Theme
Humans are long gone. Honeybees have evolved into the Mellifera — a hyper-intelligent spacefaring civilization. You control a faction of these space bees, exploring planets, gathering resources, developing technologies, and creating carvings before the Dearth (winter) arrives.

#### Your Turn — Pick One of Two Options
Every turn you do exactly one thing:

**Option A: PLACE a Worker**
1. Before placing, you may play any seed cards from your hand for their printed benefit, or discard them for 1 basic resource each.
2. Take 1 worker from your Active Pool and set it on one of the 6 action areas on the board.
3. If the space is already occupied, the existing worker gets **bumped** (more on that below).
4. Resolve the action — your worker's strength (1–4) determines how powerful the effect is.
5. If you end up with more resources than your hive can store, discard the extras. For each discarded resource, you advance 1 step on the Queen's Favor track.

**Option B: RETRIEVE Workers**
1. Scoop up ALL your workers — from the board AND from your Landing Area — back into your Active Pool.
2. For each retrieved worker at strength 1–3: collect income from one of your farm tiles (each farm can only pay once per retrieve), then bump that worker's strength up by +1.
3. For each retrieved worker at strength 4: it immediately **hibernates** — it's done working for good.

#### Worker Strength (1–4)
Your worker cubes have numbers on their faces. Higher strength means stronger actions and access to exclusive strength-4 bonuses. Workers gain strength whenever they're bumped or retrieved. But when a strength-4 worker would gain another level, it hibernates instead.

#### Bumping — No Space Is Ever Blocked
When you place on an occupied space:
- **Strength 1–3 worker**: The owner chooses — increase +1 strength and go to Active Pool, OR keep current strength and go to Landing Area.
- **Strength 4 worker**: Immediately hibernates. No choice.

Getting bumped can actually help you — a free strength increase without spending a retrieve turn.

#### Hibernation
When a strength-4 worker would increase:
1. Return the worker cube to the supply (gone for the rest of the game).
2. Place one of your hibernation tokens into a vacant Hibernation Comb pod.
3. Gain that pod's printed benefit.

#### Game End
The game ends when all Hibernation Comb sectors for your player count are filled, OR when any single player places their 7th hibernation token. Each remaining player takes 1 final turn, then you score.

**Checkpoint:** Before your first turn, confirm everyone understands the Place/Retrieve choice, how bumping works (strength 1–3 gets a choice, strength 4 hibernates), and that worker strength maxes at 4 before hibernation.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Step by Step
1. Place the board with the correct side for your player count (1–3 or 4–5).
2. Shuffle planet tiles face-down. Place explore tokens on explore grid spaces. Set the QueenShip on its starting location.
3. Shuffle farm, recruit, and development tiles into 3 separate face-down stacks. Flip 3 face-up in each row on the Advance area.
4. Place carving tiles on Carve action spaces. Place dance tiles on Convert spaces.
5. Shuffle the seed card deck. Organize the resource supply within reach.
6. Each player takes a docking mat. Draft hive mats and faction tiles using a snake draft starting from last player. Place your faction tile on its basic side.
7. Each player: set tracking tokens on the score track (starting VP depends on turn order — 1st seat = 1 VP, 2nd = 2 VP, etc.), place 7 hibernation tokens on your docking mat, take starting workers and resources per your faction tile. Deal 2 seed cards to each player — keep 1, discard the other.

#### The Six Actions

**EXPLORE** — Move the shared QueenShip orthogonally up to 1 space per total strength of ALL workers sitting on Explore spaces. You must end on a different planet. If the destination has an explore token, gain it and reveal a new planet tile. Place 1 basic resource on the planet if there's space, then collect all resources sitting on that planet. **Strength-4 bonus:** Also gain the planet's printed strength-4 benefit.

**ADVANCE** — Pick up 1 tile from the Farm, Recruit, or Development display. Which column you can reach depends on the combined strength of workers on Advance: 2+ = leftmost only, 3+ = left or middle, 5+ = any column. Pay the resource cost (Farms cost Fiber/Water, Recruits cost Pollen, Developments cost Wax), then build the tile on an empty hex on your hive mat adjacent to an existing tile. **Strength-4 bonus:** Gain 3 VP.

**GROW** — Spend your worker's strength as points, combined with resources, to grow your hive. New worker: 1 Pollen + 1 strength point (you can never have more than 4 active workers). Frame extension: 2 basic resources + 2 strength points. **Strength-4 bonus:** Flip your faction tile to its upgraded side — this is the *only* way to upgrade.

**RESEARCH** — Draw seed cards equal to your worker's strength, but keep only 1 — discard the rest. You can play kept cards later for one-time benefits or discard them for 1 basic resource. **Strength-4 bonus:** Plant 1 seed card from your hand — tuck it under your hive mat for end-game scoring. Planting slots: 2 base + 1 per frame (first 2 frames only) = 4 maximum.

**CONVERT** — Perform resource swaps up to your worker's strength number of times. Options: swap 1 basic resource for another, pay Pollen + Fiber for Wax, pay 2 Pollen + Water for Honey, discard/draw a seed card, or use any taught dance. **Strength-4 bonus:** Teach a dance (once per game per player) — you create a new conversion option available to everyone. When opponents use your dance, you gain Queen's Favor.

**CARVE** — Requires a strength-4 worker. Pay the honey cost for a carving tile and place it on an empty hive hex. Carvings score big at game end based on specific conditions. The supply is limited and never replenished — there's real pressure to grab the best ones early.

#### Hive Mat & Resource Storage
- You place tiles adjacent to existing tiles on your hexagonal hive mat.
- Covering hexes with build bonus icons grants you immediate benefits.
- Filling every base hex on your hive = 8 VP at endgame.
- Completing a frame (all 4 hexes filled) = 8 VP at endgame per frame.
- Resources sit on designated hive spots with limited capacity. Farms add more storage.
- Excess resources at end of turn get discarded; each discard advances your Queen's Favor by 1. You may NOT voluntarily discard just to gain Queen's Favor.

#### Queen's Favor Track
You advance it by discarding excess resources and through crown icons on various components. The VP scale escalates — the farther you climb, the more each step is worth. Resources left in your hive at game end do NOT convert to Queen's Favor.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
The game ends when EITHER:
- All Hibernation Comb sectors for your player count are filled, OR
- Any single player places their 7th hibernation token.

Each remaining player takes 1 final turn, then you score.

#### All VP Sources

**During the game** (already tracked on the score track):
- Explore token bonuses, farm income, hibernation pod benefits, build bonuses, strength-4 bonuses, tile ability activations, seed card benefits, starting VP

**End-game scoring** (add these up now):
- Planted seed card conditions
- Farms: 1 VP each
- Recruits: variable VP as printed on the tile
- Developments: variable VP as printed
- Carving tile conditions (this is often your biggest single source if you've met the conditions)
- Queen's Favor track position (escalating scale — top spots are worth a lot)
- Filled hive mat: 8 VP
- Completed frames: 8 VP each
- Faction endgame ability (from your upgraded side, if you upgraded)
- Hibernation Comb sector majorities (most tokens in a sector scores VP; ties share, rounded down)

#### Tiebreaker
Most active workers on board or docking mat. If still tied: shared victory.

#### Typical Scores
- A solid game lands around ~100 VP.
- If someone scores above ~150, double-check the rules — common culprits are worker aging errors, seed card planting limits, or a game that ran too long.
- A normal game lasts roughly 18 turns per player.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Master Bump Timing
When your worker gets bumped, it gains a free strength increase — no retrieve turn needed. Position your workers so opponents bump you at useful times. Conversely, bumping an opponent's strength-4 worker forces it into hibernation, costing them a worker.

#### Plan Your Strength-4 Turns
Strength-4 workers unlock the most powerful bonuses in the game: the Carve action, faction upgrade via Grow, planting seed cards via Research, teaching dances via Convert, and +3 VP on Advance. Map out your worker aging so you squeeze maximum value from these premium turns before hibernation claims each worker.

#### Get Wax and Honey Without Wasting Convert Turns
Advanced resources (Wax for Developments, Honey for Carvings) can only come from conversion or special effects — never directly from planets. Any tile, seed card, or explore token that hands you Wax or Honey for free is a massive efficiency gain. Prioritize these whenever you spot them.

#### Know When to Retrieve
Retrieving at the right moment is critical. If you've got 2–3 farm tiles, a well-timed retrieve can flood you with income. But every retrieve turn is a turn you're not placing a worker. Aim to retrieve when you have 2–3 workers on the board and good farms to collect from.

#### Complete Your Hive
8 VP for filling all base hexes and 8 VP per completed frame adds up fast. Don't leave awkward gaps — prioritize tiles that fill those holes, even if another tile looks flashier.

#### Commit to Queen's Favor or Skip It
The track has escalating returns — dabbling gives mediocre points. Either go all-in (especially if you snag the Retinue carving tile, which can be worth up to 50 VP from the track alone) or focus your scoring elsewhere entirely.

#### Seed Cards Are Powerful — But Read the Fine Print
Playing seed cards can swing the game dramatically. Acquiring them outside the Research action (via explore tokens or dances) is a strong efficiency play. But remember: Research draws cards equal to your strength, yet you keep exactly 1. Don't waste Research actions at low strength.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Forgetting Research keeps only 1 card** — You draw equal to strength, but keep exactly 1. This is the most commonly misplayed rule in Apiary.
- **Voluntarily discarding for Queen's Favor** — You cannot. Only end-of-turn storage overflow triggers the track.
- **Missing the faction upgrade** — Strength-4 on Grow is the ONLY way to flip your faction tile. Don't overlook it or you'll miss your endgame ability entirely.
- **Ignoring hibernation strategy** — Choose Comb pods for both their immediate benefit AND sector majority positioning. A pod that gives you 2 Pollen isn't worth much if it loses you a 10 VP majority.
- **Late engine building** — Farms and recruits bought in Rounds 1–2 compound over many turns. Bought late, they barely pay off.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Apiary

**What You Start With**: A hive mat with your faction tile on its basic side, a docking mat with workers in your Active Pool, some starting resources on your hive's storage spots, and 1 seed card. On the shared board you'll see 6 action areas and the exploration map with the QueenShip.

**Early Turns (1–4)**: Focus on Explore and Advance. Explore moves the QueenShip to a new planet — you'll pick up resources and explore tokens along the way. Advance lets you buy farm, recruit, or development tiles to start filling your hive. Farms are especially valuable early because they give you both storage AND income every time you retrieve.

**Your First Retrieve**: After placing 2–3 workers, take a Retrieve turn. Scoop up all your workers from the board. For each one at strength 1–3, collect income from one of your farms, then bump its strength by +1. Your workers come back stronger — that's the engine warming up.

**Mid-Game (Turns 5–12)**: Keep building your hive with tiles. Start converting basic resources into Wax (for developments) and Honey (for carvings). Watch your workers' strength — when one hits 4, plan to use its premium bonus before it hibernates. Strength-4 on Grow upgrades your faction tile. Strength-4 on Research lets you plant a seed card for end-game scoring. Don't waste these moments.

**The Carving Phase**: Once you have Honey and a strength-4 worker, head to the Carve action. Carving tiles are your ticket to major end-game points — but they're limited and never come back. Grab the ones that match your hive layout before opponents snatch them.

**Late Game (Turns 13–18)**: Workers are hibernating regularly now. Place your hibernation tokens strategically in the Comb — pick pods for both their immediate benefit and to secure sector majorities. The game ends when the Comb fills or someone places their 7th token.

**Scoring**: Add up all in-game VP + planted seed conditions + tile VP + carving conditions + Queen's Favor + hive completion + frame completion + faction ability + Comb majorities. First game target: 70–90 VP is solid. 100+ is excellent.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Hibernation Timing
Sending bees to hibernate is Apiary's defining decision. Hibernate too early and you lose actions; too late and you miss strength-4 bonuses entirely. **Optimal pattern**: use each worker for 2–3 placements, trigger its strength-4 bonus on the most impactful action available, then let it hibernate into a strategically chosen Comb pod.

#### Hive Layout Planning
Your hive mat has limited hexes. Before you start building, look at which build-bonus icons are printed on covered hexes — placing a tile there gives you an immediate reward. Plan adjacencies so farms cluster near your resource storage and carvings land on bonus hexes. Filling every base hex (8 VP) and each frame (8 VP) is a huge chunk of your score.

#### Faction Tile Asymmetry
Your faction's basic side gives you a starting ability, but the upgraded side (flipped via strength-4 on Grow) often defines your entire endgame. For example, some factions score VP per tile type, others gain resources on hibernation. Study your upgraded side early and build toward it.

#### Resource Conversion Efficiency
Basic resources (Pollen, Fiber, Water) flow freely from planets. Advanced resources don't — Wax costs Pollen + Fiber, Honey costs 2 Pollen + Water. Every Convert turn you skip by getting advanced resources from tile abilities, seed cards, or explore tokens is a turn you can spend placing workers instead. The Advance-row recruits that produce Wax passively are among the strongest tiles in the game.

#### Carving Rush vs. Engine First
Carvings are limited and never refill. If you see a carving that perfectly matches your hive (like one that scores per farm when you're farm-heavy), racing to Honey and a strength-4 worker to grab it is worth disrupting your engine. But grabbing a carving that doesn't fit your build just because it's available is a trap — you've spent Honey and a strength-4 turn for maybe 5 VP.

#### Dance Floor Leverage
Teaching a dance (strength-4 on Convert, once per game) creates a conversion anyone can use. The trick: every time an opponent uses your dance, you gain Queen's Favor. Teach a dance that opponents actually want — like a cheap Honey conversion — and you'll passively climb the track while they do the work.

#### Comb Sector Majorities
Don't just hibernate into the highest-benefit pod. Check which sectors are contested. Placing 2 tokens in an uncontested sector can lock up 8–12 VP in majority bonuses. Placing a 3rd token where you already lead is usually wasteful — spread out.

rulings: Research: draw cards equal to worker strength, but keep exactly 1. This is the most commonly misplayed rule.
Bumped workers at strength 1-3: owner chooses between +1 strength to Active Pool or same strength to Landing Area. Strength-4 bumped workers hibernate immediately with no choice.
You cannot voluntarily discard resources for Queen's Favor. Only end-of-turn storage overflow triggers it.
One tile per Advance action — combined strength only determines which columns you can access, not how many tiles you take.
Strength-4 on Grow is the only way to upgrade your faction tile. This is a crucial bonus often missed by new players.
Dance teaching is once per game per player. When opponents use your dance, you gain Queen's Favor.
Only basic resources (Pollen, Fiber, Water) can be placed on planets during Explore. Wax and Honey cannot.
The faction tile does NOT count as a farm, recruit, development, or carving for scoring or condition purposes.
Workers already in the Active Pool during a Retrieve turn do NOT gain strength, hibernate, or collect income.
If you begin your turn with zero workers everywhere, you gain 1 strength-1 worker free (Empty Hive Rule).
